steps.title: Clear fractions
steps.content: Multiply both sides of $\frac{1}{cx}=\frac{x}{80}+\frac{1}{c}$ by $80cx$: $$80=cx^2+80x.$$ So the equation becomes $$cx^2+80x-80=0.$$

steps.title: Solve the discriminant equation
steps.content: A quadratic has exactly one real solution when its discriminant is $0$. Here the discriminant is $$80^2-4(c)(-80)=80^2+4c(80).$$ Set the discriminant equal to $0$ and solve to get $c=-20$.

steps.title: Confirm the answer
steps.content: Solving by hand gives $$80^2+320c=0$$ $$6{,}400+320c=0$$ $$c=-20.$$ The answer is -20.
